checklist.item: Check passport validity (6+ months beyond return date)
Confirm Schengen entry requirements for your passport
Book Sagrada Família tickets online (sells out weeks ahead)
Purchase travel insurance with medical cover
Download offline Google Maps for Barcelona
Get a Revolut / Wise card to avoid ATM fees

days.title: Day 1 (June 15) — ARRIVAL & EL BORN
days.content:
### **Day 1 (June 15) — ARRIVAL & EL BORN**

**Getting In**
Amsterdam → Barcelona El Prat (BCN) — ~2h flight. Take the **Aerobus** (€6.75, 35 min) to Plaça Catalunya. Skip the taxi.

**Where to Sleep**
Stay in **El Born** or **Gothic Quarter** — puts you walking distance from everything Day 1-3. Budget €75-90/night for a decent private room.

**Afternoon**
Drop your bags and walk the Barri Gòtic without a map. Find the **Temple d'August** (free, hidden inside the Centre Excursionista de Catalunya building) — it's a genuine 1st-century Roman temple and most visitors walk right past it.

**Evening**
- **El Xampanyet** (Carrer de Montcada) — oldest cava bar in Barcelona, €2.50/glass, great anchovies
- **Bar del Pla** for dinner: *croquetes*, *patates braves*, solid wine list. ~€25pp
- Walk Passeig del Born after dark — the neighbourhood comes alive.

💡 **Book NOW:** Sagrada Família for Day 2 at 9am (tower access add-on worth it). They sell out weeks ahead. €26-32 online.

days.title: Day 2 (June 16) — GAUDÍ DAY
days.content:
### **Day 2 (June 16) — GAUDÍ DAY**

**Morning: Sagrada Família (9am)**
First entry, arrive 10 min early. Budget 2-2.5 hours — the interior is genuinely overwhelming. Tower access (€5 extra) gives you the best view over the Eixample grid. Cost: ~€26-32.

**Midday: Park Güell**
Metro to Vallcarca or Lesseps (20 min walk up). **Book the monumental zone** in advance (€10) — the terrace and mosaic esplanade are the reason you're going. Free park around it is also beautiful. Grab a *bocadillo* from a stall outside.

**Afternoon: Gràcia**
Walk down into Gràcia — Barcelona's most liveable neighbourhood. **Carrer de Verdi**, **Plaça del Sol**, **Mercat de l'Abaceria** (local market). Browse vintage shops on Travessera de Gràcia.

**Evening**
- **Bar Calders** (Carrer del Parlament, Eixample Esquerra) — pintxos + wine ~€20pp
- Walk the **Passeig de Gràcia** at night to see Casa Batlló and Casa Milà lit up (free, just the exterior)

💡 **Marco's tip:** Avoid eating anywhere within 200m of La Rambla.

days.title: Day 3 (June 17) — BARCELONETA & EL POBLENOU
days.content:
### **Day 3 (June 17) — BARCELONETA & EL POBLENOU**

**Morning: Barceloneta Beach**
Go before 10am while it's still breathable. Walk the **Passeig Marítim** south to Port Olímpic and back. Take a swim if the forecast is clear.

**Midday: La Cova Fumada**
The best paella/rice in Barcelona, cash only, no reservations, seats ~30 people. **Arrive at 1pm exactly** when they open — queue forms fast. Order the *arròs amb bogavant* or whatever the daily special is. ~€16-20pp.

**Afternoon: El Poblenou**
Barcelona's former industrial district, now creative hub. Walk the **Rambla del Poblenou** (the locals' Rambla — no tourists). Coffee at **Federal Café**. Wander the side streets: street art, design studios, converted warehouses.

**Evening: El Born Nightlife**
Back to El Born by 8pm. The neighbourhood transforms at night.
- **Bar Marsella** (Carrer dels Escudellers) — Barcelona's oldest bar, absinthe and atmosphere since 1820
- **Cactus Bar** for mezcal
- Or just follow the sound of music down Carrer del Rec

Budget €15-20 for drinks.

days.title: Day 4 (June 18) — MONTJUÏC & POBLE SEC
days.content:
### **Day 4 (June 18) — MONTJUÏC & POBLE SEC**

**Morning: Montjuïc**
Take the **cable car** up from Barceloneta (€12.70 return) — the views over the port and city are excellent. Visit **Fundació Joan Miró** (€14) — genuinely great contemporary art, 1.5-2 hours max. Skip the castle unless you love Spanish military fortresses.

**Midday: Carrer de Blai**
Walk down to Poble Sec. **Carrer de Blai** is Barcelona's best pintxos street — €1-1.50 per pintxo, choose the ones with fresh seafood or jamón. Go at 1pm before the lunch rush fills the street.

**Afternoon: Eixample Modernisme**
Walk up to the Eixample and stroll the **Block of Discord**:
- **Casa Batlló** (€35 — worth it if architecture is your thing, skip if not)
- **Casa Amatller** — free exterior, beautiful tiling
- **Casa Lleó Morera** — most underrated of the three

**Evening: Vermouth Hour**
*La hora del vermut* — 6-8pm at any bar in Gràcia or Poble Sec. Order vermut amb sifon, olives on the side. This is the real Barcelona ritual that tourists miss.

- **Bar Calders** again, or **Bodega Sepúlveda** for wine and charcuterie
- Dinner at **Tickets** if you booked months ago (Albert Adrià tapas bar); otherwise **Bodega 1900** is more bookable.

days.title: Day 5 (June 19) — PICASSO, PALAU & DEPARTURE
days.content:
### **Day 5 (June 19) — PICASSO, PALAU & DEPARTURE**

**Morning: Museu Picasso (9am)**
Opens at 9am. €14 general admission — book online the day before. The early work is the revelation: his technical skill at 14-16 is genuinely shocking. Budget 1.5-2 hours.

**Late Morning: Palau de la Música Catalana**
Guided tour (€22, ~55 min) of the only Modernista concert hall in the world — the stained glass ceiling alone is worth it. Book online. One of the most beautiful interiors you'll see anywhere.

**Lunch**
Last meal at **Mercado del Born** — the cultural centre built over a 1714 archaeological site. Great cafés inside. Order *pa amb tomàquet* with jamón ibéric.

**Afternoon**
Final wander. Buy gifts at any of the design shops on Carrer dels Flassaders. Last *café amb llet* at **Bar del Pi** on Plaça de Sant Josep Oriol.

**Airport**
Aerobus from Plaça Catalunya (€6.75, 35 min). Allow 2 hours before departure.

---

## Budget Summary
| Category | Estimated |
|---|---|
| Flights (AMS ↔ BCN) | €185 |
| Accommodation (5 nights ~€80/night) | €400 |
| Food & drink | €220 |
| Activities & entrance fees | €120 |
| Local transport | €45 |
| **Total** | **€970** |

✅ **€230 under your €1,200 budget** — use it for Casa Batlló, a nicer dinner, or shopping.

messages.content:
Plan my trip with these details:
- Destination: Barcelona, Spain
- Traveling from: Amsterdam
- Dates: 2026-06-15 to 2026-06-19 (5 days)
- Total budget: 1200 EUR
- Travel style: Culture & History, Food & Drink
- Dietary preferences: No restrictions
- Driver's license: Yes

Please generate a complete day-by-day itinerary.

Barcelona in June — great call. Five days is the sweet spot: enough to get past the tourist surface without running out of things to discover. I've pulled real flight and hotel data so the numbers below are actual, not guesswork.

---
